world.title: 4 Indian Tourists Caught Stealing Hotel Items From Resort In Bali's Ubud; Viral Video Shows Luggage Check By Staff
world.description: Four Indian tourists were caught allegedly taking hotel items from a luxury resort in Bali, with a video of staff checking their luggage going viral. Items like towels, robes and utensils were recovered during checkout. The matter was later settled amicably, with all belongings returned and no police complaint filed.

world.title: Ex-abbot of Thai temple jailed 50 years over 2bil baht embezzlement scandal linked to online gambling network
world.description: BANGKOK: A Thai court has sentenced a former abbot of Wat Rai Khing to 50 years in prison after finding him guilty in a major embezzlement case involving more than two billion baht (US$62.14 million) in temple funds, with money trails linked to online gambling networks and close associates.

business.title: RBI updates e-mandate norms: No OTP needed for recurring payments up to ₹15,000 - here's what it means for users
business.description: RBI on Tuesday issued a consolidated digital payments e-mandate framework, setting a threshold of ₹15,000 per transaction for recurring payments without requiring an OTP. The framework includes provisions for cross-border payments and mandates pre-debit alerts, among other measures. Details here.

ai.title: Anthropic’s Mythos AI reportedly accessed by outsiders days after launch - here’s what happened
ai.description: A reported breach involving Anthropic’s Mythos highlights a growing paradox in AI, tools built to strengthen cybersecurity may themselves become targets. As companies race to develop powerful models, controlling access is proving just as critical as innovation, especially when even limited releases can be quietly exposed.
